other_observers: Brandon Percival, the entire DFO field trip of 17 people, plus Gene Rutherford; Brandon found this bird first
specific_location: Chico Basin Ranch at the area known as the small HQ Pond with its justly famous migrant trap there.
description: This was a spectacled vireo. It had a noticeable greenish back. From the side, its head was a bluish gray that contrasted with the dull greenish back. The flanks were yellowish that contrasted with the very white underparts. The undertail coverts were white and the tail was grayish on the central rects and greenish on the outer ones. The face was blue gray and where it had contact with the white chin and throat there was a distinct demarcation with the blue-gray face, not an indistinct one.Behaviors: Sluggishly moved about, foraging in the blossoms of some low willows, eventually showing all of the characteristcs listed above.Call: none heardPlumage: alternate
